```{r parameter-definitions, echo=FALSE}
parameter_definitions <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c("boundary", "tau", "beta", "drift_con", "gain", "loss", "update", "decay"),
  Type = c("DDM", "DDM", "DDM", "DDM", "RL", "RL", "RL", "RL"),
  Definition = c(
    "Decision threshold - higher values indicate more cautious decision-making requiring more evidence",
    "Non-decision time - cognitive processing time unrelated to the decision process",
    "Response bias - tendency to favor one response option over another",
    "Drift rate consistancy - Influence of learning on drift rate",
    "Reward sensitivity",
    "Punishment sensitivity",
    "Value updating rate - how quickly expected values are updated based on new information",
    "Memory decay rate - how quickly prior information is forgotten"
  )
)

kable(parameter_definitions, caption = "Decision Model Parameter Definitions") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

# Hypothesis-Driven Model Comparison with AICc and FDR Correction
# -------------------------------------------------------------

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(AICcmodavg)
  library(texreg)
  library(kableExtra)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
})

#' Visualize parameters for each supported model individually
#' 
#' @param results Results from analyze_hypothesis_outcome
#' @param outcome The risk outcome to visualize
#' @return List of ggplot objects
visualize_supported_models_individually <- function(results, outcome) {
  if (length(results$models) == 0 || nrow(results$supported_models) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get supported models
  supported_model_names <- results$supported_models$Modnames
  
  # Create a list to store plots for each model
  model_plots <- list()
  
  # For each supported model, create a parameter plot
  for (model_name in supported_model_names) {
    # Extract model information
    model_row <- results$supported_models[results$supported_models$Modnames == model_name, ]
    hypothesis <- model_row$Hypothesis
    aicc_weight <- model_row$AICcWt
    
    # Extract parameters from this model
    param_data <- results$param_results[results$param_results$model_name == model_name, ]
    
    if (nrow(param_data) == 0) {
      next
    }
    
    # Create parameter groupings for visualization
    param_data$param_group <- sapply(param_data$parameter, function(param) {
      if (grepl("^gain|^loss", param)) return("Motivation")
      if (grepl("^update|^decay|^retend", param)) return("Learning")
      if (grepl("^boundary|^tau|^beta", param)) return("DDM/Decision")
      if (grepl("^drift_con$", param)) return("Evidence (Hybrid)")
      if (grepl("^con$", param)) return("Evidence (RL)")
      if (grepl("^good_|^bad_|^money", param)) return("Performance")
      if (grepl(":male$", param)) return("Gender Interaction")
      if (grepl("^male$", param)) return("Gender")
      if (grepl("^grade12|^time_since_puberty", param)) return("Developmental")
      return("Other")
    })
    
    # Create cleaner parameter labels
    param_data$param_label <- sapply(param_data$parameter, function(param) {
      # For interactions, format as "Parameter × Gender"
      if (grepl(":male$", param)) {
        base_param <- gsub(":male$", "", param)
        return(paste0(base_param, " × Gender"))
      }
      return(param)
    })
    
    # Order parameter groups
    group_order <- c("Performance", "Motivation", "Learning", "DDM/Decision", 
                     "Evidence (RL)", "Evidence (Hybrid)", "Gender Interaction", 
                     "Gender", "Developmental", "Other")
    param_data$param_group <- factor(param_data$param_group, levels = group_order)
    
    # Create visualization for this model
    p <- ggplot(param_data, 
                aes(x = param_label, y = estimate)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - std_error, ymax = estimate + std_error), 
                    width = 0.25) +
      geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(significant, "*", ""), y = ifelse(estimate >= 0, 
                                                                     estimate + std_error, 
                                                                     estimate - std_error)), 
                vjust = ifelse(param_data$estimate >= 0, -0.5, 1.5)) +
      facet_wrap(~param_group, scales = "free_x") +
      labs(title = paste("Parameter Estimates -", outcome),
           subtitle = paste0(hypothesis, " Hypothesis (AICc weight = ", round(aicc_weight, 3), ")"),
           x = "Parameter", 
           y = "Standardized Estimate") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    
    model_plots[[model_name]] <- p
  }
  
  return(model_plots)
}

#' Build hypothesis-driven models for each outcome and model type
#' 
#' @param outcome The risk outcome variable name
#' @param df The dataframe containing the data
#' @param model_type String identifying the model type ("behavioral", "rl", "hybrid")
#' @return List containing models and their formulas
build_hypothesis_models <- function(outcome, df, model_type) {
  # Initialize model list and formula tracking
  models <- list()
  model_formulas <- list()
  
  # Base formula components
  random_effects <- "(1|schoolid)"
  
  # 1. Behavioral Models
  if (model_type == "behavioral") {
    # Define hypotheses
    hypotheses <- list(
      individual_play = "good_play_ratio",
      individual_choice = "good_choice_ratio",
      money_only = "money",
      play_behavior = "good_play_ratio + bad_play_ratio",
      choice_performance = "good_choice_ratio + bad_play_ratio",
      full_performance = "good_choice_ratio + bad_play_ratio + money"
    )
  }
  
  # 2. RL Models
  else if (model_type == "rl") {
    # Check which parameters are available in the model
    available_params <- colnames(df)[colnames(df) %in% model_defaults[[full_model_name]]$params]
    
    # Check for update/decay parameters
    cognitive_params <- available_params[available_params %in% c("update", "decay", "retend")]
    cognitive_string <- paste(cognitive_params, collapse = " + ")
    
    # Define hypotheses
    hypotheses <- list(
      motivation = "gain + loss",
      cognitive = cognitive_string,
      motivation_cognitive = paste("gain + loss +", cognitive_string),
      full_rl = paste(available_params, collapse = " + ")
    )
  }
  
  # 3. Hybrid Models
  else if (model_type == "hybrid") {
    # Check which parameters are available in the model
    available_params <- colnames(df)[colnames(df) %in% model_defaults[[hybrid_full_model_name]]$params]
    
    # Identify parameter groups
    boundary_params <- available_params[grepl("^boundary", available_params)]
    boundary_string <- paste(boundary_params, collapse = " + ")
    
    ddm_params <- available_params[available_params %in% c("boundary", "tau", "beta", "boundary1", "tau1")]
    ddm_string <- paste(ddm_params, collapse = " + ")
    
    cognitive_params <- available_params[available_params %in% c("update", "decay", "retend")]
    cognitive_string <- paste(cognitive_params, collapse = " + ")
    
    # Define hypotheses
    hypotheses <- list(
      boundary_only = boundary_string,
      full_ddm = ddm_string,
      motivation = "gain + loss",
      cognitive = cognitive_string,
      ddm_motivation = paste(ddm_string, "+ gain + loss"),
      ddm_cognitive = paste(ddm_string, "+", cognitive_string),
      full_hybrid = paste(available_params, collapse = " + ")
    )
  }
  
  # Build models for each hypothesis
  for (hyp_name in names(hypotheses)) {
    param_string <- hypotheses[[hyp_name]]
    
    # Skip if parameter string is empty
    if (param_string == "") next
    
    # 1. Basic model (no controls)
    formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~", param_string, "+", random_effects)
    model_name <- paste(outcome, model_type, hyp_name, "basic", sep="_")
    models[[model_name]] <- try_fit_model(formula_str, df)
    model_formulas[[model_name]] <- formula_str
    
    # 2. With gender as control
    formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~", param_string, "+ male +", random_effects)
    model_name <- paste(outcome, model_type, hyp_name, "gender", sep="_")
    models[[model_name]] <- try_fit_model(formula_str, df)
    model_formulas[[model_name]] <- formula_str
    
    # 3. With gender interactions for motivation parameters (only for relevant hypotheses)
    if (grepl("gain|loss", param_string)) {
      # Create formula with gender interactions only for motivation parameters
      other_params <- gsub("gain \\+ loss", "", param_string)
      if (other_params != "") {
        formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~ (gain + loss) * male +", other_params, "+", random_effects)
      } else {
        formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~ (gain + loss) * male +", random_effects)
      }
      model_name <- paste(outcome, model_type, hyp_name, "gender_int", sep="_")
      models[[model_name]] <- try_fit_model(formula_str, df)
      model_formulas[[model_name]] <- formula_str
    }
    
    # 4. With developmental controls
    formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~", param_string, "+ grade12 + time_since_puberty +", random_effects)
    model_name <- paste(outcome, model_type, hyp_name, "dev", sep="_")
    models[[model_name]] <- try_fit_model(formula_str, df)
    model_formulas[[model_name]] <- formula_str
    
    # 5. Full controls (gender + developmental)
    formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~", param_string, "+ male + grade12 + time_since_puberty +", random_effects)
    model_name <- paste(outcome, model_type, hyp_name, "full", sep="_")
    models[[model_name]] <- try_fit_model(formula_str, df)
    model_formulas[[model_name]] <- formula_str
    
    # 6. Full controls with gender interaction for motivation
    if (grepl("gain|loss", param_string)) {
      # Create formula with gender interactions only for motivation parameters
      other_params <- gsub("gain \\+ loss", "", param_string)
      if (other_params != "") {
        formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~ (gain + loss) * male +", other_params, 
                             "+ grade12 + time_since_puberty +", random_effects)
      } else {
        formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~ (gain + loss) * male", 
                             "+ grade12 + time_since_puberty +", random_effects)
      }
      model_name <- paste(outcome, model_type, hyp_name, "full_gender_int", sep="_")
      models[[model_name]] <- try_fit_model(formula_str, df)
      model_formulas[[model_name]] <- formula_str
    }
  }
  
  # Return the list of models and their formulas
  return(list(models = models, formulas = model_formulas))
}

#' Compare models and extract parameter information with FDR correction
#' 
#' @param outcome The risk outcome variable name
#' @param df The dataframe containing the data
#' @param model_type String identifying the model type ("behavioral", "rl", "hybrid")
#' @return List containing model comparison and parameter results
analyze_hypothesis_outcome <- function(outcome, df, model_type) {
  # Build models
  model_results <- build_hypothesis_models(outcome, df, model_type)
  models <- model_results$models
  model_formulas <- model_results$formulas
  
  # Skip if no models were successfully built
  if (length(models) == 0) {
    return(list(
      outcome = outcome,
      model_type = model_type,
      models = list(),
      aicc_results = data.frame(),
      param_results = data.frame(),
      formulas = list(),
      supported_models = data.frame()
    ))
  }
  
  # Convert model names to vector for AICc comparison
  model_names <- names(models)
  
  # Compare models using AICc
  aictab_input <- models
  aicc_result <- aictab(cand.set = aictab_input, modnames = model_names)
  aicc_df <- as.data.frame(aicc_result)
  
  # Add model formulas to AICc table for reference
  aicc_df$Formula <- unlist(model_formulas[match(aicc_df$Modnames, model_names)])
  
  # Extract hypothesis names from model names
  aicc_df$Hypothesis <- sapply(aicc_df$Modnames, function(name) {
    parts <- strsplit(name, "_")[[1]]
    if (length(parts) >= 4) {
      return(parts[3])  # Extract hypothesis name from model name
    } else {
      return(NA)
    }
  })
  
  # Extract control type from model names
  aicc_df$Control <- sapply(aicc_df$Modnames, function(name) {
    parts <- strsplit(name, "_")[[1]]
    if (length(parts) >= 5) {
      return(parts[4])  # Extract control type from model name
    } else {
      return("basic")
    }
  })
  
  # Identify models with substantial support (AICc weight > 0.1)
  supported_models <- aicc_df[aicc_df$AICcWt > 0.1, ]
  
  # Calculate hypothesis weights (sum of AICc weights for each hypothesis)
  hypothesis_weights <- tapply(aicc_df$AICcWt, aicc_df$Hypothesis, sum)
  hypothesis_weights_df <- data.frame(
    Hypothesis = names(hypothesis_weights),
    TotalWeight = as.numeric(hypothesis_weights)
  )
  hypothesis_weights_df <- hypothesis_weights_df[order(hypothesis_weights_df$TotalWeight, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  # Extract parameter estimates and p-values for FDR correction
  # We'll organize by hypothesis to apply FDR within each hypothesis group
  param_results <- data.frame(
    outcome = character(),
    model_type = character(),
    model_name = character(),
    hypothesis = character(),
    parameter = character(),
    estimate = numeric(),
    std_error = numeric(),
    t_value = numeric(),
    p_value = numeric(),
    aicc_weight = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (i in seq_along(models)) {
    model_name <- model_names[i]
    model <- models[[i]]
    
    # Extract hypothesis name
    hypothesis <- NA
    parts <- strsplit(model_name, "_")[[1]]
    if (length(parts) >= 4) {
      hypothesis <- parts[3]
    }
    
    # Get AICc weight
    aicc_weight <- aicc_df$AICcWt[aicc_df$Modnames == model_name]
    
    if ("lmerModLmerTest" %in% class(model)) {
      coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients
      p_col <- "Pr(>|t|)"
    } else {
      coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients
      p_col <- ifelse("Pr(>|t|)" %in% colnames(coefs), "Pr(>|t|)", 4)
    }
    
    # Extract model parameters (excluding intercept and standard controls)
    param_rows <- rownames(coefs)
    param_rows <- param_rows[!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)$|^schoolid", param_rows)]
    
    for (param in param_rows) {
      param_results <- rbind(param_results, data.frame(
        outcome = outcome,
        model_type = model_type,
        model_name = model_name,
        hypothesis = hypothesis,
        parameter = param,
        estimate = coefs[param, "Estimate"],
        std_error = coefs[param, "Std. Error"],
        t_value = coefs[param, "t value"],
        p_value = coefs[param, p_col],
        aicc_weight = aicc_weight,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
  
  # Apply FDR correction to p-values within each hypothesis
  if (nrow(param_results) > 0) {
    # Initialize p_adjusted column
    param_results$p_adjusted <- NA
    param_results$significant <- FALSE
    
    # Apply FDR correction within each hypothesis
    for (hyp in unique(param_results$hypothesis)) {
      if (is.na(hyp)) next
      
      hyp_rows <- which(param_results$hypothesis == hyp)
      param_results$p_adjusted[hyp_rows] <- p.adjust(param_results$p_value[hyp_rows], method = "fdr")
      param_results$significant[hyp_rows] <- param_results$p_adjusted[hyp_rows] < 0.05
    }
  }
  
  # Return results
  return(list(
    outcome = outcome,
    model_type = model_type,
    models = models,
    aicc_results = aicc_df,
    param_results = param_results,
    formulas = model_formulas,
    supported_models = supported_models,
    hypothesis_weights = hypothesis_weights_df
  ))
}

#' Visualize parameters from supported models (AICc weight > 0.1)
#' 
#' @param results Results from analyze_hypothesis_outcome
#' @param outcome The risk outcome to visualize
#' @return ggplot object
visualize_supported_models <- function(results, outcome) {
  if (length(results$models) == 0 || nrow(results$supported_models) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get supported models
  supported_model_names <- results$supported_models$Modnames
  
  # Extract parameters from supported models
  param_data <- results$param_results[results$param_results$model_name %in% supported_model_names, ]
  
  if (nrow(param_data) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create parameter groupings for visualization
  param_data$param_group <- sapply(param_data$parameter, function(param) {
    if (grepl("^gain|^loss", param)) return("Motivation")
    if (grepl("^update|^decay|^retend", param)) return("Learning")
    if (grepl("^boundary|^tau|^beta", param)) return("DDM/Decision")
    if (grepl("^drift_con$", param)) return("Evidence (Hybrid)")
    if (grepl("^con$", param)) return("Evidence (RL)")
    if (grepl("^good_|^bad_|^money", param)) return("Performance")
    if (grepl(":male$", param)) return("Gender Interaction")
    if (grepl("^male$", param)) return("Gender")
    if (grepl("^grade12|^time_since_puberty", param)) return("Developmental")
    return("Other")
  })
  
  # Create cleaner parameter labels
  param_data$param_label <- sapply(param_data$parameter, function(param) {
    # For interactions, format as "Parameter × Gender"
    if (grepl(":male$", param)) {
      base_param <- gsub(":male$", "", param)
      return(paste0(base_param, " × Gender"))
    }
    return(param)
  })
  
  # Add model label for visualization
  param_data$model_label <- sapply(param_data$model_name, function(name) {
    parts <- strsplit(name, "_")[[1]]
    if (length(parts) >= 5) {
      return(paste(parts[3], parts[4], sep="_"))  # Hypothesis + control type
    } else if (length(parts) >= 4) {
      return(paste(parts[3], "basic", sep="_"))
    } else {
      return(name)
    }
  })
  
  # Create visualization
  ggplot(param_data, 
         aes(x = param_label, y = estimate, fill = model_label)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - std_error, ymax = estimate + std_error), 
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.25) +
    facet_wrap(~param_group, scales = "free_x") +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(significant, "*", ""), y = ifelse(estimate >= 0, 
                                                                   estimate + std_error, 
                                                                   estimate - std_error)), 
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), vjust = ifelse(param_data$estimate >= 0, -0.5, 1.5)) +
    labs(title = paste("Parameter Estimates for", outcome, "- Supported Models (AICc weight > 0.1)"),
         subtitle = "* indicates significance after FDR correction",
         x = "Parameter", 
         y = "Standardized Estimate",
         fill = "Model") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
}

#' Create gender interaction plots for significant interactions
#' 
#' @param results Results from analyze_hypothesis_outcome
#' @param outcome The risk outcome to visualize
#' @return List of ggplot objects
create_interaction_plots <- function(results, outcome) {
  if (length(results$models) == 0 || nrow(results$supported_models) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get supported models
  supported_model_names <- results$supported_models$Modnames
  
  # Find significant gender interactions in supported models
  interaction_data <- results$param_results[
    results$param_results$model_name %in% supported_model_names & 
      results$param_results$significant & 
      grepl(":male$", results$param_results$parameter), ]
  
  if (nrow(interaction_data) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Initialize list to store plots
  interaction_plots <- list()
  
  # For each significant interaction, create visualization
  for (i in 1:nrow(interaction_data)) {
    interaction <- interaction_data[i, ]
    model_name <- interaction$model_name
    parameter <- gsub(":male$", "", interaction$parameter)
    model <- results$models[[model_name]]
    
    # Extract hypothesis and control type for plot title
    parts <- strsplit(model_name, "_")[[1]]
    hypothesis <- parts[3]
    control_type <- ifelse(length(parts) >= 5, parts[4], "basic")
    
    # Create data for interaction plot
    if ("lmerModLmerTest" %in% class(model)) {
      fixed_effects <- fixef(model)
    } else {
      fixed_effects <- coef(model)
    }
    
    # Find main effect and interaction terms
    main_effect <- fixed_effects[parameter]
    interaction_effect <- fixed_effects[paste0(parameter, ":male")]
    intercept <- fixed_effects["(Intercept)"]
    male_effect <- ifelse("male" %in% names(fixed_effects), fixed_effects["male"], 0)
    
    # Create plotting data
    plot_data <- data.frame(
      param_value = c(-1, 0, 1), # Standardized parameter values
      female = intercept + main_effect * c(-1, 0, 1),
      male = intercept + male_effect + (main_effect + interaction_effect) * c(-1, 0, 1)
    )
    
    # Reshape for plotting
    plot_data_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(plot_data, cols = c("female", "male"), 
                                          names_to = "gender", values_to = "outcome_value")
    
    # Create plot
    p <- ggplot(plot_data_long, aes(x = param_value, y = outcome_value, color = gender, group = gender)) +
      geom_line() +
      geom_point() +
      labs(title = paste("Gender Interaction for", parameter, "on", outcome),
           subtitle = paste("Hypothesis:", hypothesis, "| Control:", control_type,
                            "| AICc weight:", round(interaction$aicc_weight, 3)),
           x = parameter, 
           y = outcome,
           color = "Gender") +
      theme_minimal()
    
    interaction_plots[[paste(model_name, parameter, sep="_")]] <- p
  }
  
  return(interaction_plots)
}

# Run analysis for all outcomes and model types
# --------------------------------------------

# Define risk outcomes
risk_outcomes <- c("bsss_sd", "ysr_attentionproblems", "ysr_maggressive_t", "ysr_mconduct_t")

# Run analyses
cat("\n# Hypothesis-Driven Model Comparison Analysis\n")

behavioral_results <- list()
rl_results <- list()
hybrid_results <- list()

for (outcome in risk_outcomes) {
  cat(paste0("\nAnalyzing models for: ", outcome, "...\n"))
  behavioral_results[[outcome]] <- analyze_hypothesis_outcome(outcome, behav_final_df, "behavioral")
  rl_results[[outcome]] <- analyze_hypothesis_outcome(outcome, rl_final_df, "rl")
  hybrid_results[[outcome]] <- analyze_hypothesis_outcome(outcome, hybrid_final_df, "hybrid")
}

# Display results
cat("\n# Model Comparison Results\n")

for (outcome in risk_outcomes) {
  cat(paste0("\n## Models for ", outcome, "\n"))
  
  # Function to display model results with hypothesis weights
  display_model_results <- function(results, model_type) {
    cat(paste0("\n### ", model_type, " Models\n"))
    
    if (length(results$models) == 0 || nrow(results$aicc_results) == 0) {
      cat("No models were successfully built for this outcome and model type.\n")
      return()
    }
    
    # Display hypothesis weights
    cat("\n#### Hypothesis Evidence Weights\n")
    kable(results$hypothesis_weights, digits = 4, 
          caption = paste("Evidence Weights for Hypotheses -", model_type, "Models")) %>%
      kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
      print()
    
    # Display supported models (AICc weight > 0.1)
    cat("\n#### Supported Models (AICc weight > 0.1)\n")
    if (nrow(results$supported_models) > 0) {
      kable(results$supported_models[, c("Modnames", "Hypothesis", "Control", "K", "AICc", "Delta_AICc", "AICcWt")], 
            caption = paste("Supported", model_type, "Models for", outcome),
            digits = 4) %>%
        kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
        print()
      
      # Display formulas for supported models
      cat("\n**Model formulas:**\n\n")
      for (i in 1:nrow(results$supported_models)) {
        model_name <- results$supported_models$Modnames[i]
        formula <- results$supported_models$Formula[i]
        cat(paste0("- **", model_name, "**: ", formula, "\n"))
      }
    } else {
      cat("No models had substantial support (AICc weight > 0.1) for this outcome and model type.\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Display results for each model type
  display_model_results(behavioral_results[[outcome]], "Behavioral")
  display_model_results(rl_results[[outcome]], "RL")
  display_model_results(hybrid_results[[outcome]], "Hybrid")
}

# Parameter estimates for supported models
cat("\n# Parameter Estimates for Supported Models\n")

for (outcome in risk_outcomes) {
  cat(paste0("\n## Parameters for ", outcome, "\n"))
  
  # Function to display parameter estimates for supported models
  display_parameter_estimates <- function(results, model_type) {
    cat(paste0("\n### ", model_type, " Parameters\n"))
    
    if (length(results$models) == 0 || nrow(results$supported_models) == 0) {
      cat("No supported models for this outcome and model type.\n")
      return()
    }
    
    # Get supported models
    supported_model_names <- results$supported_models$Modnames
    
    # Extract significant parameters from supported models
    significant_params <- results$param_results[
      results$param_results$model_name %in% supported_model_names & 
        results$param_results$significant, ]
    
    if (nrow(significant_params) > 0) {
      # Add model information for display
      significant_params$hypothesis <- sapply(significant_params$model_name, function(name) {
        parts <- strsplit(name, "_")[[1]]
        if (length(parts) >= 4) {
          return(parts[3])
        } else {
          return(NA)
        }
      })
      
      significant_params$control <- sapply(significant_params$model_name, function(name) {
        parts <- strsplit(name, "_")[[1]]
        if (length(parts) >= 5) {
          return(parts[4])
        } else {
          return("basic")
        }
      })
      
      # Display table of significant parameters
      kable(significant_params[, c("parameter", "hypothesis", "control", "estimate", 
                                   "std_error", "p_value", "p_adjusted", "aicc_weight")], 
            caption = paste("Significant Parameters in Supported", model_type, "Models for", outcome),
            digits = 4) %>%
        kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
        print()
    } else {
      cat("No significant parameters after FDR correction in supported models.\n")
    }
    
    # Visualize parameters from supported models
    # Visualize parameters from supported models
    cat("\n#### Parameter Visualization\n")
    model_plots <- visualize_supported_models_individually(results, outcome)
    if (!is.null(model_plots) && length(model_plots) > 0) {
      for (model_name in names(model_plots)) {
        print(model_plots[[model_name]])
      }
    } else {
      cat("No parameter plots available for supported models.\n")
    }
    
    # Create gender interaction plots
    interaction_plots <- create_interaction_plots(results, outcome)
    if (!is.null(interaction_plots) && length(interaction_plots) > 0) {
      cat("\n#### Gender Interaction Plots\n")
      for (i in 1:length(interaction_plots)) {
        print(interaction_plots[[i]])
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Display parameter estimates for each model type
  display_parameter_estimates(behavioral_results[[outcome]], "Behavioral")
  display_parameter_estimates(rl_results[[outcome]], "RL")
  display_parameter_estimates(hybrid_results[[outcome]], "Hybrid")
}

# Theoretical Implications Section
cat("\n# Theoretical Implications\n")

# For each hypothesis, check evidence across outcomes
all_hypothesis_weights <- data.frame()

for (outcome in risk_outcomes) {
  # Behavioral
  if (length(behavioral_results[[outcome]]$models) > 0 && 
      nrow(behavioral_results[[outcome]]$hypothesis_weights) > 0) {
    hyp_weights <- behavioral_results[[outcome]]$hypothesis_weights
    hyp_weights$outcome <- outcome
    hyp_weights$model_type <- "Behavioral"
    all_hypothesis_weights <- rbind(all_hypothesis_weights, hyp_weights)
  }
  
  # RL
  if (length(rl_results[[outcome]]$models) > 0 && 
      nrow(rl_results[[outcome]]$hypothesis_weights) > 0) {
    hyp_weights <- rl_results[[outcome]]$hypothesis_weights
    hyp_weights$outcome <- outcome
    hyp_weights$model_type <- "RL"
    all_hypothesis_weights <- rbind(all_hypothesis_weights, hyp_weights)
  }
  
  # Hybrid
  if (length(hybrid_results[[outcome]]$models) > 0 && 
      nrow(hybrid_results[[outcome]]$hypothesis_weights) > 0) {
    hyp_weights <- hybrid_results[[outcome]]$hypothesis_weights
    hyp_weights$outcome <- outcome
    hyp_weights$model_type <- "Hybrid"
    all_hypothesis_weights <- rbind(all_hypothesis_weights, hyp_weights)
  }
}

if (nrow(all_hypothesis_weights) > 0) {
  # Create a hypothesis evidence heatmap across outcomes
  hypothesis_matrix <- reshape2::acast(all_hypothesis_weights, 
                                       Hypothesis ~ model_type + outcome, 
                                       value.var = "TotalWeight")
  
  # Replace NAs with zeros
  hypothesis_matrix[is.na(hypothesis_matrix)] <- 0
  
  # Display hypothesis evidence weights across outcomes
  cat("\n## Hypothesis Evidence Across Outcomes\n")
  
  # Convert matrix to data frame for kable
  hypothesis_df <- as.data.frame(hypothesis_matrix)
  hypothesis_df$Hypothesis <- rownames(hypothesis_matrix)
  hypothesis_df <- hypothesis_df[, c(ncol(hypothesis_df), 1:(ncol(hypothesis_df)-1))]
  
  kable(hypothesis_df, digits = 3,
        caption = "Evidence Weights for Each Hypothesis Across Outcomes") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE) %>%
    print()
  
  # Create a heatmap for hypothesis evidence
  hyp_heatmap_data <- reshape2::melt(hypothesis_matrix)
  names(hyp_heatmap_data) <- c("Hypothesis", "Model_Outcome", "Weight")
  
  # Split Model_Outcome into separate columns
  hyp_heatmap_data <- tidyr::separate(hyp_heatmap_data, Model_Outcome, 
                                      into = c("Model", "Outcome"), sep = "_")
  
  # Plot heatmap
  ggplot(hyp_heatmap_data, aes(x = Outcome, y = Hypothesis, fill = Weight)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    facet_wrap(~ Model, scales = "free_x") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
    labs(title = "Hypothesis Evidence Across Outcomes",
         x = "Risk Outcome", 
         y = "Hypothesis",
         fill = "Evidence Weight") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}

# Collect significant parameters across outcomes
all_significant_params <- data.frame()

for (outcome in risk_outcomes) {
  # Only include parameters from supported models (AICc weight > 0.1)
  
  # Behavioral
  if (length(behavioral_results[[outcome]]$models) > 0 && 
      nrow(behavioral_results[[outcome]]$supported_models) > 0) {
    
    supported_models <- behavioral_results[[outcome]]$supported_models$Modnames
    significant_params <- behavioral_results[[outcome]]$param_results[
      behavioral_results[[outcome]]$param_results$model_name %in% supported_models &
        behavioral_results[[outcome]]$param_results$significant, ]
    
    if (nrow(significant_params) > 0) {
      significant_params$outcome <- outcome
      all_significant_params <- rbind(all_significant_params, significant_params)
    }
  }
  
  # RL
  if (length(rl_results[[outcome]]$models) > 0 && 
      nrow(rl_results[[outcome]]$supported_models) > 0) {
    
    supported_models <- rl_results[[outcome]]$supported_models$Modnames
    significant_params <- rl_results[[outcome]]$param_results[
      rl_results[[outcome]]$param_results$model_name %in% supported_models &
        rl_results[[outcome]]$param_results$significant, ]
    
    if (nrow(significant_params) > 0) {
      significant_params$outcome <- outcome
      all_significant_params <- rbind(all_significant_params, significant_params)
    }
  }
  
  # Hybrid
  if (length(hybrid_results[[outcome]]$models) > 0 && 
      nrow(hybrid_results[[outcome]]$supported_models) > 0) {
    
    supported_models <- hybrid_results[[outcome]]$supported_models$Modnames
    significant_params <- hybrid_results[[outcome]]$param_results[
      hybrid_results[[outcome]]$param_results$model_name %in% supported_models &
        hybrid_results[[outcome]]$param_results$significant, ]
    
    if (nrow(significant_params) > 0) {
      significant_params$outcome <- outcome
      all_significant_params <- rbind(all_significant_params, significant_params)
    }
  }
}

if (nrow(all_significant_params) > 0) {
  # Find parameters that are significant across multiple outcomes
  param_counts <- table(all_significant_params$parameter)
  multi_outcome_params <- names(param_counts[param_counts > 1])
  
  if (length(multi_outcome_params) > 0) {
    cat("\n## Parameters Significant Across Multiple Outcomes\n")
    
    for (param in multi_outcome_params) {
      param_data <- all_significant_params[all_significant_params$parameter == param, ]
      outcomes_affected <- unique(param_data$outcome)
      
      cat(paste0("- **", param, "** significantly predicts: ", 
                 paste(outcomes_affected, collapse = ", "), "\n"))
      
      for (outcome in outcomes_affected) {
        outcome_data <- param_data[param_data$outcome == outcome, ]
        for (i in 1:nrow(outcome_data)) {
          row <- outcome_data[i, ]
          effect_dir <- ifelse(row$estimate > 0, "positive", "negative")
          model_type <- row$model_type
          hyp_name <- row$hypothesis
          
          cat(paste0("  - ", outcome, " (", model_type, ", ", hyp_name, " hypothesis): ", 
                     effect_dir, " effect (β = ", round(row$estimate, 3), 
                     ", p-adjusted = ", round(row$p_adjusted, 3), 
                     ", AICc weight = ", round(row$aicc_weight, 3), ")\n"))
        }
      }
      cat("\n")
    }
  }
}

# Create a parameter significance heatmap
if (nrow(all_significant_params) > 0) {
  # Create parameter groups for better visualization
  all_significant_params$param_group <- sapply(all_significant_params$parameter, function(param) {
    if (grepl("^gain|^loss", param)) return("Motivation")
    if (grepl("^update|^decay|^retend", param)) return("Learning")
    if (grepl("^boundary|^tau|^beta", param)) return("DDM/Decision")
    if (grepl("^drift_con$", param)) return("Evidence (Hybrid)")
    if (grepl("^con$", param)) return("Evidence (RL)")
    if (grepl("^good_|^bad_|^money", param)) return("Performance")
    if (grepl(":male$", param)) {
      base_param <- gsub(":male$", "", param)
      if (grepl("^gain|^loss", base_param)) return("Motivation × Gender")
      if (grepl("^update|^decay|^retend", base_param)) return("Learning × Gender")
      if (grepl("^boundary|^tau|^beta", base_param)) return("DDM/Decision × Gender")
      return("Other × Gender")
    }
    if (grepl("^male$", param)) return("Gender")
    if (grepl("^grade12|^time_since_puberty", param)) return("Developmental")
    return("Other")
  })
  
  # Create cleaner parameter labels
  all_significant_params$clean_parameter <- sapply(all_significant_params$parameter, function(param) {
    if (grepl(":male$", param)) {
      base_param <- gsub(":male$", "", param)
      return(paste0(base_param, " × Gender"))
    }
    return(param)
  })
  
  # Create data for heatmap
  param_effect_data <- all_significant_params %>%
    dplyr::group_by(model_type, outcome, clean_parameter, param_group) %>%
    dplyr::summarize(
      avg_estimate = mean(estimate),
      max_aicc_weight = max(aicc_weight),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  # Set consistent parameter group order
  group_order <- c("Performance", "Motivation", "Learning", "DDM/Decision", 
                   "Evidence (RL)", "Evidence (Hybrid)", "Motivation × Gender", 
                   "Learning × Gender", "DDM/Decision × Gender", "Other × Gender",
                   "Gender", "Developmental", "Other")
  param_effect_data$param_group <- factor(param_effect_data$param_group, levels = group_order)
  
  # Plot heatmap
  cat("\n## Parameter Significance Patterns\n")
  
  # Create heatmap of effect sizes
  ggplot(param_effect_data, 
         aes(x = outcome, y = clean_parameter, fill = avg_estimate)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_text(aes(label = "*", 
                  color = ifelse(abs(avg_estimate) > 0.5, "white", "black")),
              size = 5) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    facet_grid(param_group ~ model_type, scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
    labs(title = "Parameter Effects Across Risk Outcomes",
         subtitle = "* indicates significance after FDR correction (AICc weight > 0.1)",
         x = "Risk Outcome", 
         y = "Parameter",
         fill = "Effect Size") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
          strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0),
          panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines"))
}

# Theoretical discussion of findings
cat("\n## Key Theoretical Findings\n\n")

cat("Based on our hypothesis-driven analysis, we can draw several theoretical conclusions about the computational mechanisms underlying adolescent risk behaviors:\n\n")

# Check if motivation parameters are important across outcomes
if (nrow(all_significant_params) > 0) {
  motivation_params <- all_significant_params[grepl("^gain|^loss", all_significant_params$parameter), ]
  if (nrow(motivation_params) > 0) {
    motivation_outcomes <- unique(motivation_params$outcome)
    
    cat("### Motivation Parameters\n\n")
    cat(paste0("Reward sensitivity parameters (gain, loss) significantly predict ", 
               ifelse(length(motivation_outcomes) > 1, 
                      paste0("multiple risk behaviors: ", paste(motivation_outcomes, collapse = ", ")), 
                      paste0("risk behavior: ", motivation_outcomes)), 
               ".\n\n"))
    
    # Examine gain vs loss patterns
    gain_params <- motivation_params[grepl("^gain", motivation_params$parameter), ]
    loss_params <- motivation_params[grepl("^loss", motivation_params$parameter), ]
    
    if (nrow(gain_params) > 0) {
      gain_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(gain_params$estimate) > 0, "positively", "negatively")
      cat(paste0("- **Reward sensitivity (gain)** ", gain_effect_dir, " predicts risk behaviors, suggesting that ",
                 ifelse(mean(gain_params$estimate) > 0, 
                        "adolescents who are more sensitive to rewards tend to engage in more risk behaviors.",
                        "adolescents who are less sensitive to rewards tend to engage in more risk behaviors."),
                 "\n\n"))
    }
    
    if (nrow(loss_params) > 0) {
      loss_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(loss_params$estimate) > 0, "positively", "negatively")
      cat(paste0("- **Punishment sensitivity (loss)** ", loss_effect_dir, " predicts risk behaviors, suggesting that ",
                 ifelse(mean(loss_params$estimate) > 0, 
                        "adolescents who are more sensitive to punishments tend to engage in more risk behaviors.",
                        "adolescents who are less sensitive to punishments tend to engage in more risk behaviors."),
                 "\n\n"))
    }
  }
  
  # Check cognitive parameters
  cognitive_params <- all_significant_params[grepl("^update|^decay|^retend", all_significant_params$parameter), ]
  if (nrow(cognitive_params) > 0) {
    cognitive_outcomes <- unique(cognitive_params$outcome)
    
    cat("### Learning Parameters\n\n")
    cat(paste0("Learning parameters (update, decay) significantly predict ", 
               ifelse(length(cognitive_outcomes) > 1, 
                      paste0("multiple risk behaviors: ", paste(cognitive_outcomes, collapse = ", ")), 
                      paste0("risk behavior: ", cognitive_outcomes)), 
               ".\n\n"))
    
    # Examine specific learning parameter patterns
    update_params <- cognitive_params[grepl("^update", cognitive_params$parameter), ]
    decay_params <- cognitive_params[grepl("^decay", cognitive_params$parameter), ]
    
    if (nrow(update_params) > 0) {
      update_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(update_params$estimate) > 0, "positively", "negatively")
      cat(paste0("- **Learning rate (update)** ", update_effect_dir, " predicts risk behaviors, suggesting that ",
                 ifelse(mean(update_params$estimate) > 0, 
                        "adolescents who update their value expectations more quickly tend to engage in more risk behaviors.",
                        "adolescents who update their value expectations more slowly tend to engage in more risk behaviors."),
                 "\n\n"))
    }
    
    if (nrow(decay_params) > 0) {
      decay_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(decay_params$estimate) > 0, "positively", "negatively")
      cat(paste0("- **Memory decay** ", decay_effect_dir, " predicts risk behaviors, suggesting that ",
                 ifelse(mean(decay_params$estimate) > 0, 
                        "adolescents who forget previous experiences more quickly tend to engage in more risk behaviors.",
                        "adolescents who maintain memory of previous experiences longer tend to engage in more risk behaviors."),
                 "\n\n"))
    }
  }
  
  # Check DDM parameters
  ddm_params <- all_significant_params[grepl("^boundary|^tau|^beta", all_significant_params$parameter), ]
  if (nrow(ddm_params) > 0) {
    ddm_outcomes <- unique(ddm_params$outcome)
    
    cat("### Decision Process Parameters\n\n")
    cat(paste0("Decision process parameters (boundary, tau, beta) significantly predict ", 
               ifelse(length(ddm_outcomes) > 1, 
                      paste0("multiple risk behaviors: ", paste(ddm_outcomes, collapse = ", ")), 
                      paste0("risk behavior: ", ddm_outcomes)), 
               ".\n\n"))
    
    # Examine specific DDM parameter patterns
    boundary_params <- ddm_params[grepl("^boundary", ddm_params$parameter), ]
    tau_params <- ddm_params[grepl("^tau", ddm_params$parameter), ]
    beta_params <- ddm_params[grepl("^beta", ddm_params$parameter), ]
    
    if (nrow(boundary_params) > 0) {
      boundary_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(boundary_params$estimate) > 0, "positively", "negatively")
      cat(paste0("- **Decision threshold (boundary)** ", boundary_effect_dir, " predicts risk behaviors, suggesting that ",
                 ifelse(mean(boundary_params$estimate) > 0, 
                        "adolescents who require more evidence before making decisions tend to engage in more risk behaviors.",
                        "adolescents who make decisions with less evidence tend to engage in more risk behaviors."),
                 "\n\n"))
    }
    
    if (nrow(tau_params) > 0) {
      tau_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(tau_params$estimate) > 0, "positively", "negatively")
      cat(paste0("- **Non-decision time (tau)** ", tau_effect_dir, " predicts risk behaviors, suggesting that ",
                 ifelse(mean(tau_params$estimate) > 0, 
                        "adolescents with longer cognitive processing times tend to engage in more risk behaviors.",
                        "adolescents with shorter cognitive processing times tend to engage in more risk behaviors."),
                 "\n\n"))
    }
    
    if (nrow(beta_params) > 0) {
      beta_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(beta_params$estimate) > 0, "positively", "negatively")
      cat(paste0("- **Response bias (beta)** ", beta_effect_dir, " predicts risk behaviors, suggesting that ",
                 ifelse(mean(beta_params$estimate) > 0, 
                        "adolescents with stronger response biases tend to engage in more risk behaviors.",
                        "adolescents with weaker response biases tend to engage in more risk behaviors."),
                 "\n\n"))
    }
  }
  
  # Check gender interactions
  gender_int_params <- all_significant_params[grepl(":male", all_significant_params$parameter), ]
  if (nrow(gender_int_params) > 0) {
    gender_int_outcomes <- unique(gender_int_params$outcome)
    
    cat("### Gender Differences\n\n")
    cat(paste0("Gender significantly moderates the relationship between computational parameters and ", 
               ifelse(length(gender_int_outcomes) > 1, 
                      paste0("multiple risk behaviors: ", paste(gender_int_outcomes, collapse = ", ")), 
                      paste0("risk behavior: ", gender_int_outcomes)), 
               ".\n\n"))
    
    # Examine specific gender interaction patterns
    for (param in unique(gsub(":male", "", gender_int_params$parameter))) {
      param_ints <- gender_int_params[grepl(paste0(param, ":male"), gender_int_params$parameter), ]
      if (nrow(param_ints) > 0) {
        param_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(param_ints$estimate) > 0, "stronger", "weaker")
        cat(paste0("- The relationship between **", param, "** and risk behaviors is ", param_effect_dir, 
                   " for males compared to females.\n\n"))
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Check developmental parameters
  dev_params <- all_significant_params[grepl("^grade12|^time_since_puberty", all_significant_params$parameter), ]
  if (nrow(dev_params) > 0) {
    dev_outcomes <- unique(dev_params$outcome)
    
    cat("### Developmental Effects\n\n")
    cat(paste0("Developmental factors significantly predict ", 
               ifelse(length(dev_outcomes) > 1, 
                      paste0("multiple risk behaviors: ", paste(dev_outcomes, collapse = ", ")), 
                      paste0("risk behavior: ", dev_outcomes)), 
               ".\n\n"))
    
    # Examine specific developmental parameter patterns
    grade_params <- dev_params[grepl("^grade12", dev_params$parameter), ]
    puberty_params <- dev_params[grepl("^time_since_puberty", dev_params$parameter), ]
    
    if (nrow(grade_params) > 0) {
      grade_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(grade_params$estimate) > 0, "higher", "lower")
      cat(paste0("- **Grade level** is associated with ", grade_effect_dir, " risk behaviors, suggesting developmental changes in risk-taking.\n\n"))
    }
    
    if (nrow(puberty_params) > 0) {
      puberty_effect_dir <- ifelse(mean(puberty_params$estimate) > 0, "higher", "lower")
      cat(paste0("- **Time since puberty** is associated with ", puberty_effect_dir, " risk behaviors, suggesting pubertal timing effects on risk-taking.\n\n"))
    }
  }
}
